item.title: Native Voices
item.description: Long before digital preservation or cultural funding, two women quietly reshaped the course of Native American history. Ethnomusicologist Frances Densmore, working in the early 20th century, recorded more than 2,000 tribal songs on wax cylinders using rudimentary phonograph technology. Her work documented ceremonial chants, healing songs, and oral histories from dozens of tribes—many of which were at risk of being lost to colonization and cultural suppression. Across decades, another guardian emerged: Gladys Tantaquidgeon, a Mohegan elder and traditional medicine woman. She chronicled her tribe’s ancient remedies and beliefs in handwritten notebooks. Though stored away for years, these records proved crucial when the Mohegan Tribe sought federal recognition in 1994—a process requiring deep proof of continuous cultural identity. Her documentation helped restore sovereignty and inspired a cultural renaissance marked by the founding of the Mohegan Sun. These acts of preservation were more than academic—they were acts of resistance. In a world that sought to erase Indigenous cultures, these women safeguarded what mattered most: language, ceremony, and memory. Their legacy remains a cornerstone of historical preservation and Indigenous resilience.
item.dc:creator: TheTaoofRob

item.title: GovBrief Today - What Happened #161
item.description: The pushback is becoming a roar and it's making them panic. Medical clinic staff grabbed ICE agents' hands and yelled "get off him." California protesters stared down tear gas and rubber bullets for farmworkers in unorganized protests that spread by word-of-mouth. A whistleblower dropped receipts proving Trump's judge pick told his staff to tell courts to "go fuck themselves." Florida lawmakers sued DeSantis for his detention camp access. When people see others refuse to back down, they stop backing down too. The regime's desperate polygraph tests prove they can feel us getting stronger. Keep pushing. We're reinforcing each other, and it's working. Don't rely on the algorithm to keep you informed. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you always know what's going on. Add your email at govbrief dot today, on Facebook, or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com 1. https://go.govbrief.today/camarillo-protest-carbajal 2. https://go.govbrief.today/ice-detains-man-surgery-center 3. https://go.govbrief.today/35-percent-canadian-tariffs 4. https://go.govbrief.today/adams-malaysia-ambassador 5. https://go.govbrief.today/dhs-rules-slow-flood-relief 6. https://go.govbrief.today/un-warns-millions-die 7. https://go.govbrief.today/gunmakers-lose-appeal 8. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-block-birthright-ban 9. https://go.govbrief.today/fbi-polygraphs-criticize-patel 10. https://go.govbrief.today/defense-buys-stake-rare-earths-company 11. https://go.govbrief.today/tmobile-ends-dei 12. https://go.govbrief.today/whistleblower-text-bove 13. https://go.govbrief.today/missouri-repeals-2024-voter-mandate 14. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-sexual-assault-verdict-upheld 15. https://go.govbrief.today/lawmakers-sue-desantis-access-camp 16. https://go.govbrief.today/ice-may-deport-abrego-agarcia-again 17. https://go.govbrief.today/headstart-immigrants-banned 18. https://go.govbrief.today/gmu-dei-investigation 19. https://go.govbrief.today/state-dept-layoffs-coming 20. https://go.govbrief.today/ssa-phone-delays 21. https://go.govbrief.today/protest-guides-revealed 22. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-aides-trading Yesterday's Top Story - https://go.govbrief.today/trump-not-think-pause Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/khalil-sues-20million Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Frank is definitely a morning cat
item.description:
Woke up out of a pleasant dream and couldn't figure out why.
Oh. This is how Frank pounces. It's like a cat-apult.
So, food.
I need to clean my mirror.
Yes Frank. It is morning.
Bonus Frank being....Frank
Bonus bonus photo of the full moon from last night taken from my phone at 100x zoom. Had no idea it could do that. How you all have a great Franking Friday.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: That will happen when you point out blatant inconsistencies in a person's core beliefs
item.description: I'm kind of going to do the soapbox thing here, but please take it with a grain of salt. I'm no philosopher or world leader: If the ultimate goal were to be a good person, there wouldn't be an issue here. Inconsistencies would be pointed out in the hypocritical ideology of the religious and changes would be made on their end wholeheartedly. For the religious, though, it's not about being good. Not really. It's about feeling safe. People use religion as a shield from the bad in their lives. So, when you start to poke holes in their "beliefs" their world becomes less safe. Think about it: if those inconsistencies are true, then what else must also be true, and so on and so on. The fabric of their core becomes undone at that point. That's when the claws come out or they shut down. Anything to get them safely back to home base. Our world may be unsafe, but religion is not the answer. In fact, we got to where we are largely BECAUSE of religion... because the people in power convinced the masses that the only peace and prosperity they would know would be from following an ideology that in some cases, was written and rewritten centuries ago. It wasn't about being good, though. It was about control and "safety". There is a more efficacious, and honest path, IMHO. It's called the Golden Rule: treat others the way you want to be treated. That's it. Will you feel entirely safe? Lol, no... the world is fucked up, and you're not supposed to feel safe when that's the case. That's what sparks the initiative to affect change. And that's what we desperately need right now: universal, honest, progressive change.
item.dc:creator: Johje19

item.title: Five Nights of Frank
item.description:
I showed you my toe beans. Please respond.
Frank. That's grown up food. Your kitten food is in the cupboard.
Sleepy Frank pose 1
Sleepy Frank pose 2
Sleepy Frank pose wtf.
Bonus minute of Frank doin the groomin. Good night everyone. It's almost the weekend. Hang in there. Didn't get to see Superman tonight. Ah well, we'll go Sunday.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Sticks Are All the Rage!
